quotes.verbatim_quote:
Usually these missing tones are either the 5th or the root, as mentioned before, but don’t be shocked if any other
tone is left out. The main thing to concern yourself with is learning good sounding chord forms, and learning how to
use them, and these two points are what this book is about.
quotes.why_load_bearing: States the governing principle for chord voicing throughout the method: omitting the 5th or root is normal, and ear-appeal over theoretical completeness is the priority.

quotes.verbatim_quote: The diatonic triads in the key of C are: C Dm Em F G Am B®
quotes.why_load_bearing: Establishes the foundational seven diatonic triads in C.

quotes.verbatim_quote: The diatonic 7th chords in the key of C are: C# Dm7 Em7 F?# G7 Am? Bm7b5
quotes.why_load_bearing: Lists the seven diatonic 7th chords with OCR noise intact.

quotes.verbatim_quote:
A complete list of the most commonly used diatonic chords in the key of C including the EXTENSIONS
(extensions are chords that add other diatonic notes on to the diatonic triads or diatonic 7th chords) is: (capital
Roman numerals indicate a major triad)
quotes.why_load_bearing: Defines extensions and clarifies the Roman numeral case convention.

quotes.verbatim_quote: I: C, CF, C6, C/9, C6/9, C2, C45, (C#/6, Csus, C/9sus)

quotes.why_load_bearing: Lists IV chord extensions, longest in the chapter.

quotes.verbatim_quote:
these are two examples of what we will call HARMONIC FORMULAS (meaning
progressions, that because they are so well liked, are constantly used by musicians as a basis for improvisation, compo-
sition, arranging and what have you).
quotes.why_load_bearing: Defines harmonic formulas as the book's central organizing concept.

quotes.topic: I iii IV V formula introduced
quotes.verbatim_quote:
The first one we are going to work with is: [iii TV V (1). Only triad symbols will be used for diatonic harmonic
formulas {in other words, even if a progression uses, say C/9 Em7 F/9 G7sus C?, in a general sense it is still 1 int TV V I,

even though specifically it is 1/9 iti? [V/9 V7sus 17).
quotes.why_load_bearing: Introduces the chapter formula and the rule that harmonic formulas use triad terms regardless of extensions.

quotes.verbatim_quote:
some chords, especially some very modern voicings coming up later, sound pretty terrible in low registers {that is,
on the lower frets) so let your ears be the guide in determining how low to take any progression
quotes.why_load_bearing: Practical rule: ear judgment overrides mechanical transposition when voicings become muddy.

quotes.verbatim_quote:
in the Baroque period of music
(roughly 1600-1750), mostly triads and to a lesser extent, 7th chords, suspensions and added 9ths were used. Example:

J F .
5 I ia
é
x 1 Zz x
In modern times, the 7ths and extensions are used very often, with the triads playing a lesser role.
